en: Oh give thanks to the LORD, for he is good, for his steadfast love endures forever!
zh: 你們要稱謝耶和華，因他本是良善的； 他的慈愛永遠長存。

en: Let the redeemed of the LORD say so, whom he has redeemed from trouble
zh: 願耶和華救贖的子民這樣稱謝他； 這民就是他從敵人的手裡救贖出來，

en: and gathered in from the lands, from the east and from the west, from the north and from the south.
zh: 把他們從各地， 就是從東從西、從南 從北，招聚回來的。

en: Some wandered in desert wastes, finding no way to a city to dwell in;
zh: 他們有些人在曠野、在荒漠飄流， 尋不見路往可居住的城巿。

en: hungry and thirsty, their soul fainted within them.
zh: 他們又飢又渴， 心靈疲弱。

en: Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble, and he delivered them from their distress.
zh: 他們在急難中呼求耶和華， 耶和華就救他們脫離困苦；

en: He led them by a straight way till they reached a city to dwell in.
zh: 領他們走正直的路， 使他們去到可居住的城巿。

en: Let them thank the LORD for his steadfast love, for his wondrous works to the children of men!
zh: 願人因耶和華的慈愛， 和他向世人所行的奇事稱謝他。

en: For he satisfies the longing soul, and the hungry soul he fills with good things.
zh: 因為他使乾渴的人得到滿足， 又使飢餓的人飽享美物。

en: Some sat in darkness and in the shadow of death, prisoners in affliction and in irons,
zh: 他們有些人坐在黑暗和死蔭裡， 被困苦和鐵鍊捆鎖著；

en: for they had rebelled against the words of God, and spurned the counsel of the Most High.
zh: 因為他們違背了 神的話， 藐視了至高者的旨意。

en: So he bowed their hearts down with hard labor; they fell down, with none to help.
zh: 所以 神用苦難治服他們的心； 他們跌倒，也沒有人幫助。

en: Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble, and he delivered them from their distress.
zh: 他們在急難中呼求耶和華， 耶和華就拯救他們脫離困苦。

en: He brought them out of darkness and the shadow of death, and burst their bonds apart.
zh: 他從黑暗裡和死蔭中把他們領出來， 弄斷他們的鎖鍊。

en: Let them thank the LORD for his steadfast love, for his wondrous works to the children of men!
zh: 願人因耶和華的慈愛， 和他向世人所行的奇事稱謝他。

en: For he shatters the doors of bronze and cuts in two the bars of iron.
zh: 因為他打破了銅門， 砍斷了鐵閂。

en: Some were fools through their sinful ways, and because of their iniquities suffered affliction;
zh: 他們有些人因自己的過犯成了愚妄人， 有人受苦是因為自己的罪孽。

en: they loathed any kind of food, and they drew near to the gates of death.
zh: 他們厭惡各樣食物， 就臨近死門。

en: Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble, and he delivered them from their distress.
zh: 於是他們在急難中呼求耶和華， 他就拯救他們脫離困苦。

en: He sent out his word and healed them, and delivered them from their destruction.
zh: 他發出話語醫治他們， 搭救他們脫離死亡。

en: Let them thank the LORD for his steadfast love, for his wondrous works to the children of men!
zh: 願人因耶和華的慈愛， 因他向世人所行的奇事稱謝他。

en: And let them offer sacrifices of thanksgiving, and tell of his deeds in songs of joy!
zh: 願他們以感謝為祭獻給他， 歡欣地述說他的作為。

en: Some went down to the sea in ships, doing business on the great waters;
zh: 他們有些人坐船出海， 在大海上往來經商。

en: they saw the deeds of the LORD, his wondrous works in the deep.
zh: 他們見過耶和華的作為， 和他在深海中所行的奇事。

en: For he commanded and raised the stormy wind, which lifted up the waves of the sea.
zh: 他一吩咐，狂風就颳起， 海中的波浪也高揚。

en: They mounted up to heaven; they went down to the depths; their courage melted away in their evil plight;
zh: 他們上到天上，下到深淵， 他們的心因危難而驚慌。

en: they reeled and staggered like drunken men and were at their wits' end.
zh: 他們搖搖晃晃，東倒西歪，好像醉酒的人； 他們的一切智慧都沒有用了。

en: Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble, and he delivered them from their distress.
zh: 於是他們在急難中呼求耶和華， 他就拯救他們脫離困苦。

en: He made the storm be still, and the waves of the sea were hushed.
zh: 他使狂風止息， 海浪就平靜無聲。

en: Then they were glad that the waters were quiet, and he brought them to their desired haven.
zh: 風平浪靜了，他們就歡喜； 他引領他們到他們所願去的港口。

en: Let them thank the LORD for his steadfast love, for his wondrous works to the children of men!
zh: 願人因耶和華的慈愛， 和他向世人所行的奇事稱謝他。

en: Let them extol him in the congregation of the people, and praise him in the assembly of the elders.
zh: 願他們在眾民的會中尊崇他， 在長老的集會中讚美他。

en: He turns rivers into a desert, springs of water into thirsty ground,
zh: 他使江河變為曠野， 使水泉變為乾旱無水之地。

en: a fruitful land into a salty waste, because of the evil of its inhabitants.
zh: 他使肥沃的土地變為鹹田， 都因住在那裡的居民的邪惡。

en: He turns a desert into pools of water, a parched land into springs of water.
zh: 他使曠野變為池塘， 使旱地變為水泉。

en: And there he lets the hungry dwell, and they establish a city to live in;
zh: 他使飢餓的人住在那裡， 好讓他們建造可以居住的城巿。

en: they sow fields and plant vineyards and get a fruitful yield.
zh: 他們耕種田地，栽種葡萄園， 得到豐盛的收成。

en: By his blessing they multiply greatly, and he does not let their livestock diminish.
zh: 他又賜福給他們， 使他們人口眾多， 也沒有使他們的牲畜減少。

en: When they are diminished and brought low through oppression, evil, and sorrow,
zh: 以後他們因為壓迫、患難、愁苦， 就人數減少，降為卑微。

en: he pours contempt on princes and makes them wander in trackless wastes;
zh: 他使權貴受羞辱， 使他們在荒廢無路的地方飄流。

en: but he raises up the needy out of affliction and makes their families like flocks.
zh: 他卻把貧窮的人安置在高處，免受苦難， 使他的家族繁衍，好像羊群。

en: The upright see it and are glad, and all wickedness shuts its mouth.
zh: 正直的人看見了，就歡喜； 但所有邪惡的人都必閉口無言。

en: Whoever is wise, let him attend to these things; let them consider the steadfast love of the LORD.
zh: 凡有智慧的，願他留意這些事； 願他思想耶和華的慈愛。 歌一首，大衛的詩。
